passages.title: The Secret of the Yawn
passages.source_html:
The Secret of the Yawn 


A 


When a scientist began to study yawning in the 1980s, it was difficult to convince some of his research students of the merits of ‘yawning science.’ Although it may appear quirky, his decision to study yawning was a logical extension to human beings of my research in developmental neuroscience, reported in such papers as ‘Wing-flapping during Development and Evolution.’ As a neurobehavioral problem, there is not much difference between the wing-flapping of birds and the face – and body-flapping of human yawners. 


B 


Yawning is an ancient, primitive act. Humans do it even before they are born, opening wide in the womb. Some snakes unhinge their jaws to do it. One species of penguins yawns as part of mating. Only now are researchers beginning to understand why we yawn, when we yawn and why we yawn back. A professor of cognitive neuroscience at Drexel University in Philadelphia, Steven Platek, studies the act of contagious yawning, something done only by people and other primates. 


C 


In his first experiment, he used a psychological test to rank people on their empathic feelings. He found that participants who did not score high on compassion did not yawn back. ‘We literally had people saying, “Why am I looking at people yawning?”’ Professor Platek said. ‘It just had no effect.’ 


D 


For his second experiment, he put 10 students in a magnetic resonance imaging machine as they watched video tapes of people yawning. When the students watched the videos, the part of the brain which reacted was the part scientists believe controls empathy – the posterior cingulate, in the brain’s middle rear. ‘I don’t know if it’s necessarily that nice people yawn more, but I think it’s a good indicator of a state of mind,’ said Professor Platek, ‘It’s also a good indicator if you’re empathizing with me and paying attention.’ 


E 


His third experiment is studying yawning in those with brain disorders, such as autism and schizophrenia, in which victims have difficulty connecting emotionally with others. A psychology professor at the University of Maryland, Robert Provine, is one of the few other researchers into yawning. He found the basic yawn lasts about six seconds and they come in bouts with an interval of about 68 seconds. Men and women yawn or half-yawn equally often, but men are significantly less likely to cover their mouths which may indicate complex distinction in genders. ‘A watched yawner never yawns,’ Professor Provine said. However, the physical root of yawning remains a mystery. Some researchers say it’s coordinated within the hypothalamus of the brain, the area that also controls breathing. 


F 


Yawning and stretching also share properties and may be performed together as parts of a global motor complex. But they do not always co-occur – people usually yawn when we stretch, but we don’t always stretch when we yawn, especially before bedtime. Studies by J.I.P, G.H.A. Visser and H.F. Prechtl in the early 1980s, charting movement in the developing fetus using ultrasound, observed not just yawning but a link between yawning and stretching as early as the end of the first prenatal trimester. 


G 


The most extraordinary demonstration of the yawn-stretch linkage occurs in many people paralyzed on one side of their body because of brain damage caused by a stroke. The prominent British neurologist Sir Francis Walshe noted in 1923 what when these hemiplegics yawn, they are startled and mystified to observe that their otherwise paralyzed arm rises and flexes automatically in what neurologists term an 'associated response'. Yawning apparently activates undamaged, unconsciously controlled connections between the brain and the cord motor system innervating the paralyzed limb. It is not known whether the associated response is a positive prognosis for recovery, nor whether yawning is therapeutic for reinnervation or prevention of muscular atrophy. 


H 


Clinical neurology offers other surprises. Some patients with ‘locked-in’ syndrome, who are almost totally deprived of the ability to move voluntarily, can yawn normally. The neural circuits for spontaneous yawning must exist in the brain stem near other respiratory and vasomotor centers, because yawning is performed by anencephalic who possess only the medulla oblongata. The multiplicity of stimuli of contagious yawning, by contrast, implicates many higher brain regions.

groups.title: Questions 28-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 28-32 


Complete the summary below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 28-32 on your answer sheet.


28

A psychology professor drew a conclusion after observation that it takes about six seconds to complete an average yawning which needs 28

正确答案： 28 68 seconds
Question 28

原文答案句：He found the basic yawn lasts about six seconds and they come in bouts with an interval of about 68 seconds.  

对应段落：E段  

审题：题目询问一个心理学教授观察后得出的结论，即平均打哈欠需要6秒，并且下一个哈欠来之前需要什么。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词“six seconds”和“average yawning”定位到E段中关于心理学教授Robert Provine的研究发现。  

解题思路：原文中明确提到“basic yawn lasts about six seconds”，即基础哈欠持续大约六秒钟。结合上下文和题目，可以推断此处应填入两个哈欠之间的间隔时间，对应原文"with an interval of about 68 seconds"可以确定答案为“68 seconds”。


29

before a following yawning comes. It is almost at the same frequency that male and female yawn or half, yet behavior accompanied with yawning showing a 29 in genders.

正确答案： 29 distinction##complex distinction
Question 29

原文答案句：Men and women yawn or half-yawn equally often, but men are significantly less likely to cover their mouths which may indicate complex distinction in genders.  

对应段落：E段  

审题：题目问的是男女打哈欠的频率相同，但伴随打哈欠的行为显示出性别间的什么。  

定位关键信息：利用题干中的"yawn or half-yawn equally often"和"behavior accompanied with yawning"定位到E段中关于男女打哈欠频率相同但行为有区别的句子。  

解题思路：原文明确提到男女打哈欠或半打哈欠的频率相同，但男性捂嘴打哈欠的可能性更小，这表明存在"complex distinction in genders"（复杂的性别区别），因此答案为"complex distinction"或"distinction"。


30

Some parts within the brain may affect the movement which also have something to do with 30 .

正确答案： 30 breathing
Question 30

原文答案句：However, the physical root of yawning remains a mystery. Some researchers say it’s coordinated within the hypothalamus of the brain, the area that also controls breathing.  

对应段落：E段  

审题：题目要求根据填空题干中的信息，在原文中找到与大脑某部分同时影响某种运动（yawning）并与之相关的另一项功能。  

定位关键信息：利用题干中的"within the brain"和"movement"定位到E段中关于大脑某部分控制某种运动和另一项功能的句子。  

解题思路：原文E段提到，有些研究人员认为打哈欠的生理根源仍然是个谜，但他们认为打哈欠是由大脑的某个部分协调的，这个部分也控制着呼吸。题干中的"movement"对应原文中的"yawning"，而"breathing"是与打哈欠同时由大脑同一区域控制的功能，因此答案为breathing。


31

Another finding also finds there is a link between yawn and 31 .

正确答案： 31 stretch##stretching
Question 31

原文答案句：Yawning and stretching also share properties and may be performed together as parts of a global motor complex.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是研究发现打哈欠和什么之间存在联系。

定位关键信息：利用题目的"link between yawn and"定位到F段首句，找到"Yawning and stretching also share properties and may be performed together"。

解题思路：原文明确提到打哈欠和伸展动作有共同属性，经常一起发生，因此可以确定答案为stretch或stretching。题目中的"link between"对应原文的"share properties and may be performed together"，表明两者存在关联。


32

Before a baby was born, which two can be automatically cooperating even among people whose 32 is damaged.

正确答案： 32 brain
Question 32

原文答案句：The neural circuits for spontaneous yawning must exist in the brain stem near other respiratory and vasomotor centers, because yawning is performed by anencephalic who possess only the medulla oblongata.

对应段落：H段

审题：题目问的是在婴儿出生前，这两种行为也会自动发生，即使是在那些什么受损的人中。

定位关键信息：通过题目的关键词"before a baby was born"和"damaged"可以定位到H段中关于大脑受损和婴儿发育的描述。

解题思路：原文中提到，无脑畸形的婴儿仍然可以正常打哈欠，这表明大脑的某些部分在出生前就已经能够自动合作。根据原文的描述，这些自动合作的部位位于大脑的脑干部分，因此答案是"brain"。


Questions 33-37 


Reading paragraph A-H. Which paragraph contains the following information? Write the correct letter A-H for question 33-37. 


NB You may use any letter more than once


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
33

33 The rate for yawning shows some regular patterns.
✅ 正确答案：E

原文答案句：He found the basic yawn lasts about six seconds and they come in bouts with an interval of about 68 seconds.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是哪一段提到了关于打哈欠的频率或模式显示出一些规律性。

定位关键信息：题目中的"rate for yawning shows some regular patterns"（打哈欠的频率显示出一些规律性的模式）可以定位到文章中关于打哈欠频率或模式的具体描述。

解题思路：在E段中，文章提到Robert Provine的研究发现，基本的打哈欠持续大约六秒钟，并且它们以大约68秒的间隔成批出现。这表明打哈欠的频率有一定的规律性，即每隔大约68秒就会出现一次打哈欠。因此，E段包含了关于打哈欠频率显示出规律性的信息。
34

34 Yawning is an inherent ability that appears in both animals and humans.
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：Humans do it even before they are born, opening wide in the womb. Some snakes unhinge their jaws to do it. One species of penguins yawns as part of mating. 

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目问的是“打哈欠是一种天生的能力，出现在动物和人类身上”这一信息出现在哪个段落。  

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词“inherent ability”（天生的能力）、“animals and humans”（动物和人类）定位到原文。原文中提到“Humans do it even before they are born”（人类甚至在出生前就会打哈欠）和“Some snakes unhinge their jaws to do it”（有些蛇会张嘴打哈欠），明确说明了打哈欠是动物和人类共有的天生活动。  

解题思路：根据原文B段的内容，明确指出了人类和动物（蛇、企鹅）都会打哈欠，且打哈欠是人类出生前就具备的行为，这直接对应了题目中“inherent ability that appears in both animals and humans”的描述。因此，答案为B段。
35

35 Stretching and yawning are not always going together.
✅ 正确答案：F

原文答案句：But they do not always co-occur – people usually yawn when we stretch, but we don’t always stretch when we yawn, especially before bedtime.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是“伸展和打哈欠并不总是同时发生”，需要找到描述这两者关系并不总是同步的段落。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的“Stretching and yawning”和“not always going together”来定位答案在文章中的句子。

解题思路：根据原文F段中的句子“they do not always co-occur – people usually yawn when we stretch, but we don’t always stretch when we yawn, especially before bedtime.”，明确说明了伸展和打哈欠并不总是同时发生，尤其是睡前。这与题目要求的信息完全一致，因此答案为F段。
36

36 Yawning may suggest people are having positive notice or response in communicating.
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：It’s also a good indicator if you’re empathizing with me and paying attention.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是哪个段落提到了打哈欠可能表明人们在交流中具有积极的注意或回应。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是“positive notice or response in communicating”，在文章中寻找与“打哈欠”和“交流中的积极注意或回应”相关的句子。

解题思路：根据题目中的关键词，在D段中找到了“it’s also a good indicator if you’re empathizing with me and paying attention”这句话，意思是“它（打哈欠）也是你是否在对我表示同情和注意力的一个良好指标”。这句话表明打哈欠可以作为一种信号，表明人们在交流中是否在积极注意或回应对方，因此D段包含了题目所要求的信息。
37

37 Some superior areas in brain may deal with the infectious feature of yawning.
✅ 正确答案：H

原文答案句：The multiplicity of stimuli of contagious yawning, by contrast, implicates many higher brain regions.

对应段落：H段

审题：题目问的是“大脑的一些高级功能区可能处理打哈欠的传染性特征”，即询问关于打哈欠传染性与高级脑区相关的内容。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"superior areas in brain"（大脑的高级功能区）和"contagious"（传染性）来定位。

解题思路：在H段中，原文提到“传染性哈欠的多种刺激，相比之下，涉及到许多高级脑区”，这里的"higher brain regions"对应题目的"superior areas in brain"，而"contagious yawning"直接对应题目的"contagious feature of yawning"，因此H段包含了题目所询问的信息。

Questions 38-40 


Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage 3? In boxes 38-40 on your answer sheet, write YES if the statement is true NO if the statement is false NOT GIVEN if the information is not given in the passage


38

Several students in Platek’s experiment did not comprehend why their tutor asked them to yawn back.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：  

‘We literally had people saying, “Why am I looking at people yawning?”’ Professor Platek said. ‘It just had no effect.’  

对应段落：C段  

审题：题目说的是“在Platek的实验中，有几位学生不理解为什么他们的导师让他们打哈欠回应”，即实验对象是否对实验要求感到困惑。  

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词“students”、“comprehend”、“tutor asked them to yawn back”定位到C段。原文提到实验中有些参与者不理解为什么看别人打哈欠，但并未明确说明他们不理解导师让他们打哈欠回应。  

解题思路：原文C段提到参与者不理解“为什么看别人打哈欠”（Why am I looking at people yawning?），但题目说的是“不理解为什么导师让他们打哈欠回应”（tutor asked them to yawn back）。原文并未明确说明导师让他们打哈欠回应，也未说明学生对这一要求感到困惑，因此信息未直接对应。题目中的“tutor asked them to yawn back”在原文中没有明确支持，属于未给出信息。


39

Some results from certain experiment indicate the link between yawning and compassion.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：He found that participants who did not score high on compassion did not yawn back. ‘We literally had people saying, “Why am I looking at people yawning?”’ Professor Platek said. ‘It just had no effect.’

对应段落：C段

审题：题目说的是“某些实验的某些结果表明了打哈欠和同情心之间的联系。”

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词“yawning”和“compassion”可以定位到C段。

解题思路：C段中提到，Platek教授的实验发现，那些在同情心测试中得分不高的参与者不会回打哈欠。这说明打哈欠和同情心之间存在一定的联系，即同情心高的人更容易受到他人打哈欠的影响而回打哈欠。因此，题目中的陈述与文章信息一致，答案为A（True）。


40

Yawning can show an affirmative impact on the recovery from brain damage brought by a stroke.

正确答案： B. NO

原文答案句：It is not known whether the associated response is a positive prognosis for recovery, nor whether yawning is therapeutic for reinnervation or prevention of muscular atrophy.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目说的是“打哈欠对因中风造成的脑损伤的恢复有积极影响”。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词“recovery from brain damage brought by a stroke”定位到G段。

解题思路：原文中提到“associated response”（相关反应）是否是恢复的积极预兆，以及打哈欠是否对神经再支配或预防肌肉萎缩有疗效，目前尚不清楚。因此，题目中“打哈欠对脑损伤的恢复有积极影响”这一说法在原文中没有得到证实，属于未提及信息。
